TreeNode* helper(vector<string>& data, int& index, int min, int max) {
    if (index >= data.size() || data[index] == "#") {
        index++;
        return nullptr;
    }
    int val = stoi(data[index]);
    if (val < min || val > max) {
        return nullptr;

    TreeNode* node = new TreeNode(val);
    index++;
    node->left = helper(data, index, min, val);
    node->right = helper(data, index, val, max);
    return node;
}

class Codec {
private:
    vector<string> split(const string& s) {
        vector<string> res;
        stringstream ss(s);
        string token;
        while (getline(ss, token, ',')) {
            res.push_back(token);
        }
        return res;
    }

    TreeNode* helper(const vector<string>& data, int& index, int min, int max) {
        if (index >= data.size() || data[index] == "#") {
            index++;
            return nullptr;
        }
        int val = stoi(data[index]);
        if (val < min || val > max) {
            index++;
            return nullptr;
        }
        TreeNode* node = new TreeNode(val);
        index++;
        node->left = helper(data, index, min, val);
        node->right = helper(data, index, val, max);
        return node;
    }

public:
    string serialize(TreeNode* root) {
        if (root == nullptr) return "";
        vector<string> tokens;
        serializeHelper(root, tokens);
        return join(tokens, ",");
    }

    TreeNode* deserialize(string data) {
        if (data.empty()) return nullptr;
        vector<string> nodes = split(data);
        int index = 0;
        return helper(nodes, index, INT_MIN, INT_MAX);
    }

    void serializeHelper(TreeNode* node, vector<string>& tokens) {
        if (node == nullptr) {
            tokens.push_back("#");
            return;
        }
        tokens.push_back(to_string(node->val));
        serializeHelper(node->left, tokens);
        serializeHelper(node->right, tokens);
    }

    string join(const vector<string>& tokens, const string& delimiter) {
        string result;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < tokens.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) {
                result += delimiter;
            }
            result += tokens[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <sstream>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <climits>
using namespace std;

struct TreeNode {
    int val;
    TreeNode *left;
    TreeNode *right;
    TreeNode(int x) : val(x), left(NULL), right(NULL) {}
};

class Codec {
private:
    vector<string> split(const string& s) {
        vector<string> res;
        stringstream ss(s);
        string token;
        while (getline(ss, token, ',')) {
            res.push_back(token);
        }
        return res;
    }

    TreeNode* helper(const vector<string>& data, int& index, int min, int max) {
        if (index >= data.size() || data[index] == "#") {
            index++;
            return nullptr;
        }
        int val = stoi(data[index]);
        if (val < min || val > max) {
            index++;
            return nullptr;
        }
        TreeNode* node = new TreeNode(val);
        index++;
        node->left = helper(data, index, min, val);
        node->right = helper(data, index, val, max);
        return node;
    }

public:
    string serialize(TreeNode* root) {
        if (root == nullptr) return "";
        vector<string> tokens;
        serializeHelper(root, tokens);
        return join(tokens, ",");
    }

    TreeNode* deserialize(string data) {
        if (data.empty()) return nullptr;
        vector<string> nodes = split(data);
        int index = 0;
        return helper(nodes, index, INT_MIN, INT_MAX);
    }

    void serializeHelper(TreeNode* node, vector<string>& tokens) {
        if (node == nullptr) {
            tokens.push_back("#");
            return;
        }
        tokens.push_back(to_string(node->val));
        serializeHelper(node->left, tokens);
        serializeHelper(node->right, tokens);
    }

    string join(const vector<string>& tokens, const string& delimiter) {
        string result;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < tokens.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) {
                result += delimiter;
            }
            result += tokens[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a pre-order traversal with null markers to efficiently serialize and deserialize the BST. This approach ensures that each node is processed exactly once, leading to linear time and space complexity. The use of bounds during deserialization ensures that the BST property is maintained, allowing for correct reconstruction of the tree.
